prompt:
Need a sortable/filterable/paginated table for the fleet dashboard. Server-rendered (we snapshot pages), so ALL the interaction state lives in a reducer I can also drive from the toolbar code later — build the logic pure and the component thin. Two files, next to run_tests.mjs: grid_logic.ts (pure TS, no React): - GridState { sortKey: string | null, sortDir: "asc" | "desc", filter: string, page: number, pageSize: number } - initGrid(pageSize = 10) -> { sortKey: null, sortDir: "asc", filter: "", page: 0, pageSize } - gridReducer(state, action), actions: * { type: "sort", key } — new key: sort by it ascending. Same key as current: flip the direction. Either way page goes back to 0. * { type: "filter", text } — set the filter, page back to 0. * { type: "page", page } — jump; floor negative input at 0 (upper clamping is applyView's job, the reducer doesn't know the row count). * { type: "next" } / { type: "prev" } — prev floors at 0, next just increments. * { type: "page-size", size } — clamp to at least 1, page back to 0. * anything else: return the SAME state object. Never mutate state. - ColumnSpec { key, label, kind: "text" | "number" }, Row = Record - applyView(rows, columns, state) -> { pageRows, totalMatching, pageCount, page }: * filter: case-insensitive substring match; a row stays if ANY listed column's value (stringified) contains the filter. Empty filter keeps everything. Number cells match as text here (filter "16" finds cpus 16). * then sort when sortKey is set: kind "number" compares numerically, "text" compares the stringified values; STABLE — rows with equal keys keep their incoming order, and that holds for desc too (invert the comparison, don't reverse the array). Missing values: "" for text, 0 for number. Do NOT mutate the rows array I pass in — sort a copy. * then paginate: pageCount = max(1, ceil(totalMatching / pageSize)); clamp state.page into [0, pageCount-1] and return the clamped value as page; pageRows is that window. data_grid.tsx: export function DataGrid({ columns, rows, caption, init? }) — hold the state with useReducer(gridReducer, init ?? initGrid()) and render through applyView. Markup contract (the harness normalizes attribute order/name-case, so prop order is yours):
one one per visible row with one
{caption}
per column, carrying aria-sort="ascending"|"descending" ONLY on the currently sorted column, containing
per column (stringified cell, class="num" on kind "number" cells) — or, when nothing matches, exactly
No matching rows

Showing {first}-{last} of {totalMatching}

Page {page+1} of {pageCount}

Status line: first/last are 1-based inclusive over the MATCHING set (so page 2 of 9 rows at size 3 reads "Showing 4-6 of 9"); when nothing matches it reads exactly "Showing 0 of 0". Acceptance: `node run_tests.mjs` green. The harness (read-only) bundles your two files with the vendored React 19.2.7 under ./vendor/node_modules via the esbuild binary on PATH and asserts renderToStaticMarkup output plus the reducer/applyView behavior directly. Offline — no npm anything. SSR only: no effects, no refs, no browser APIs. Plain TS annotations (no enums/namespaces).
notes: react 19.2.7 + react-dom 19.2.7 + scheduler 0.27.0 (production cjs builds, complete packages minus READMEs) vendored in files/vendor/node_modules. Harness bundles via esbuild binary on PATH (probed v0.28.1), NODE_PATH resolution, offline at trace time. Bundle output .bundle_out/ created and removed by the harness.
